meta.section: Defender for Office 365 — Core Email Security Policies
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/defender-office-365) — portal paths and step wording grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-02. This is a VERIFICATION playbook: the settings below are the documented Inforcer baseline values; use the steps to confirm each in the tenant and record any drift.
generalNotes: All seven policies are custom (not preset-derived). Confirm each is the intended custom object and that its paired *Rule scopes the correct recipients (Get-SafeLinksRule, Get-SafeAttachmentRule, Get-MalwareFilterRule, Get-HostedContentFilterRule, Get-HostedOutboundSpamFilterRule, Get-AntiPhishRule).

Known deviation to raise with the client as ONE conversation: Safe Attachments, Anti-Malware, and the Inbound high-confidence-phish verdict all assign quarantine tag DefaultFullAccessPolicy where Microsoft recommends AdminOnlyAccessPolicy. Self-release of malware / high-confidence phish is hard-blocked regardless, but notification and visibility permissions differ.
Custom outbound/inbound/impersonation settings match Standard or Strict preset values in most places, so protection level is generally equivalent even though the policies are custom.

requiredSettings.label: Safe Links protection
requiredSettings.value: On — URLs rewritten and checked at time of click

requiredSettings.label: Apply to messages sent within the organization
Wait for URL scanning to complete before delivering
Let users click through to the original URL

steps: In the Defender portal (security.microsoft.com), go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Safe Links (or go directly to security.microsoft.com/safelinksv2).
Select the Inforcer Safe Links policy by clicking anywhere in its row to open the details flyout.
In the URL & click protection settings section, confirm Safe Links is On with URL rewriting enabled.
Confirm: Apply Safe Links to messages sent within the organization = On; Apply real-time URL scanning = On; Wait for URL scanning to complete before delivering = On.
Confirm 'Do not allow users to click through to the original URL' is selected (AllowClickThrough = False).
Verify recipient scoping on the paired rule with Get-SafeLinksRule, and record any drift in the Drift / Change Notes column.

requiredSettings.label: Action when a limit is reached
requiredSettings.value: Restrict the user from sending mail (BlockUser)

requiredSettings.label: Bcc suspicious outbound mail
requiredSettings.value: Configured True — but NON-FUNCTIONAL in a custom policy (see steps)

steps: In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Anti-spam (security.microsoft.com/antispam).
Select the Inforcer outbound policy (Type = Custom outbound spam policy).
In Protection settings, confirm the external per-hour limit (500), internal per-hour limit (1000), and daily limit (1000).
Confirm 'Restrict the user from sending mail' is the action when a limit is reached.
Confirm Automatic forwarding rules = Off.

NOTE: Microsoft's docs state the suspicious-outbound Bcc only works in the DEFAULT outbound policy, not custom ones. This policy is custom, so BccSuspiciousOutboundMail is set but does nothing — flag to the client if they believe it provides visibility. Verify scoping via Get-HostedOutboundSpamFilterRule.
In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Safe Attachments (security.microsoft.com/safeattachmentv2).
Select the Inforcer Safe Attachments policy to open the details flyout.
In the Settings section, confirm 'Safe Attachments unknown malware response' = Block and Redirect is off.

Check the Quarantine policy: the baseline uses DefaultFullAccessPolicy; Microsoft's recommended value is AdminOnlyAccessPolicy. Self-release of malware is hard-blocked either way, but notification/visibility differs — flag as a deviation (same conversation as the Anti-Malware policy).
Verify recipient scoping via Get-SafeAttachmentRule and record any drift.

requiredSettings.label: When these file types are found
requiredSettings.value: Reject the message with an NDR (FileTypeAction: Reject)

requiredSettings.label: Admin notifications
requiredSettings.value: Internal senders: On; External senders: Off

steps: In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Anti-malware.
Select the Inforcer Anti-Malware policy to open the details flyout.
In Protection settings, confirm 'Enable the common attachments filter' = On and 'When these file types are found' = Reject the message with an NDR.
Optionally open 'Select file types' to confirm the 52-type default list is intact.
Confirm 'Enable zero-hour auto purge for malware' = On.
Confirm admin notification for internal senders is On (external Off).
Quarantine policy deviation: DefaultFullAccessPolicy vs recommended AdminOnlyAccessPolicy — same conversation as Safe Attachments. Verify scoping via Get-MalwareFilterRule.

requiredSettings.label: If a message is detected as spoof
requiredSettings.value: Move to Junk Email folder (AuthenticationFailAction: MoveToJmf)

requiredSettings.label: User impersonation protection
requiredSettings.value: Enabled — but protected senders list is EMPTY (critical gap, see steps)

steps: In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Anti-phishing (security.microsoft.com/antiphishing).
Select the Inforcer Anti-Phishing policy to open the details flyout.
On the Phishing threshold & protection section, confirm the threshold = 3 and that Mailbox intelligence and Spoof intelligence are on.

CRITICAL GAP: 'Enable users to protect' is on but has ZERO protected senders — open 'Manage senders for impersonation protection' and add the client's executives/finance/owners, or user-impersonation protection isn't protecting anyone.
Consider turning on domain impersonation protection for key partner/vendor domains (currently off) via 'Enable domains to protect'.

On the Actions section, confirm spoof action = Move to Junk Email and mailbox-intelligence action = Quarantine. Impersonation quarantine tags use DefaultFullAccessPolicy (vs the notification-enabled variant) — note for visibility. Verify scoping via Get-AntiPhishRule.

requiredSettings.label: High confidence phish — quarantine policy
requiredSettings.value: DefaultFullAccessPolicy — STRONGEST DEVIATION (Microsoft recommends AdminOnlyAccessPolicy at every tier)

requiredSettings.label: Advanced Spam Filter (ASF) settings
requiredSettings.value: All 13 Off (matches best practice; ASF is being deprecated)

steps: In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Anti-spam (security.microsoft.com/antispam).
Select the Inforcer inbound policy (Type = Custom anti-spam policy).
On the Bulk email threshold & spam properties section, confirm BCL threshold = 6 and that all ASF settings are Off.
On the Actions section, review each verdict (spam, high confidence spam, phishing, high confidence phishing, bulk) and its assigned quarantine policy.

PRIORITY FIX: High confidence phishing uses quarantine policy DefaultFullAccessPolicy — Microsoft recommends AdminOnlyAccessPolicy at every tier (Default/Standard/Strict all agree). Change this first.
Confirm Bulk and Spam verdicts use the custom 'Inforcer - LimitedAccess-RequestByUser' tag. Verify scoping via Get-HostedContentFilterRule.

requiredSettings.label: Release action preference
requiredSettings.value: Allow recipients to request a message to be released (RequestRelease)

requiredSettings.label: Additional actions
requiredSettings.value: Preview + Block sender only (bitmask 26 = BlockSender 16 + RequestRelease 8 + Preview 2)

steps: In the Defender portal, go to Email & collaboration > Policies & rules > Threat policies > Quarantine policy (in the Rules section; security.microsoft.com/quarantinePolicies).
Select the 'Inforcer - LimitedAccess-RequestByUser' custom policy.

On Recipient message access, confirm Set specific access (Advanced): release preference = 'Allow recipients to request a message to be released', and additional actions = Preview + Block sender only (no Delete, no Allow sender). This equals EndUserQuarantinePermissionsValue 26.
Confirm the Quarantine notification page has Enable selected (quarantine notifications on).
Confirm this tag is the one referenced by the Spoof action (Anti-Phishing) and the Bulk/Spam verdicts (Inbound Spam Filter).

Retention note: this object specifies 15 days, but the assigning anti-spam policy specifies 30 — per Microsoft docs the anti-spam policy's retention governs for spam-type quarantine, so 30 likely wins. Confirm if it matters to the client.
